_comment: Copy to model-config.json (or just export MODEL_NAME) and edit. Controls which Claude model scripts/summarize.py calls when compressing large connector payloads (Jenkins console logs, big Jira threads, large repo files) before they enter the main threat-modeling session's context.

_available_models.claude-sonnet-5: Default. Best cost/quality balance for extractive summarization -- this is the token-minimization lever referenced in the plugin description.
_available_models.claude-haiku-4-5-20251001: Cheapest/fastest option. Use for very high-volume connector pulls (e.g. summarizing many Jenkins builds in a loop) where summary fidelity can be slightly lower.
_available_models.claude-opus-4-8: Highest fidelity. Use for unusually dense or ambiguous source material (e.g. sparse legacy documentation) where under-summarizing would lose security-relevant detail.
_available_models.claude-fable-5: Alternative general-purpose model; swap in if your org standardizes on it instead of Sonnet.

_note: This file only controls the summarization helper script. The main threat-modeling reasoning itself runs on whatever model is driving your Claude Code / Cowork session -- switch that from your client's model picker.
